ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general coffee beverage class; no specific entry for bottled ready-to-drink coffee with milk and no sugar. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FJP covers coffee with milk as a category; no specific bottled RTD no-sugar variant exists. Closest defensible code used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for bottled ready-to-drink coffee with milk and no sugar. Left null to maintain ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard bottled serving of ready-to-drink coffee with milk is approximately 240 ml (240 g), equivalent to a single commercially sold bottle.

nutrient_source.title: Coffee, with milk, no sugar — Singapore HPB Foodo Database (D09010022)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database record D09010022 for coffee with milk, no sugar, bottled. All core macros and secondary nutrients are taken from this canonical record.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific bottled RTD coffee with milk and no sugar product. GI estimated at approximately 39 (low) based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for coffee with milk (unsweetened), which reflects the low glycaemic impact of lactose (GI ~46) diluted in a predominantly water-based beverage with minimal carbohydrate content (1.53 g/100 g). Glycemic load per 240 g serving = (39 × 1.53 × 2.4) / 100 ≈ 0.9 g, classified as very low.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (6 mg/100 g) and negligible saturated fat (0.1 g/100 g); no added sugar
hypertension.guidance: With only 6 mg sodium per 100 g and no added sugar, this unsweetened bottled coffee with milk poses no meaningful blood pressure risk; however, caffeine may cause transient acute blood pressure elevation and intake should be limited to 1–2 servings per day in hypertensive individuals.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low carbohydrate (1.53 g/100 g) and low sugar (0.92 g/100 g from milk lactose only; no added sugar); estimated GI 39 (low)
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a total carbohydrate of 1.53 g/100 g, a glycaemic load of less than 1 per standard serving, and no added sugar, this beverage has negligible glycaemic impact and is suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes; caffeine may affect insulin sensitivity in some individuals and response should be monitored.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Negligible saturated fat (0.1 g/100 g), very low sodium (6 mg/100 g), and no added sugar; coffee polyphenols (chlorogenic acids) have evidence-based cardioprotective properties
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This unsweetened bottled coffee with milk is very low in saturated fat and sodium, contains no added sugar, and provides chlorogenic acid polyphenols associated with modest cardiovascular benefit; moderate consumption of 1–2 servings per day is appropriate for individuals with cardiovascular disease, with attention to total daily caffeine intake.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Coffee polyphenols (chlorogenic acids) with anti-inflammatory properties; no added sugar; negligible saturated fat
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Coffee's chlorogenic acid content provides evidence-based anti-inflammatory benefit relevant to rheumatoid arthritis; no added sugar and negligible saturated fat mean no pro-inflammatory load, though caffeine intake above 3 cups per day may interfere with calcium absorption important for RA bone health.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Coffee with Milk, No Sugar (Bottled, Ready-to-Drink) is a commercially prepared, shelf-stable beverage consisting of brewed coffee combined with milk and no added sugar, sold in sealed bottles for immediate consumption. The beverage is very low in calories (15 kcal per 100 g), contains minimal carbohydrate (1.53 g/100 g) derived primarily from milk lactose (0.92 g sugar/100 g), a small amount of protein (0.3 g/100 g) from the milk fraction, and negligible fat (0.15 g/100 g). Sodium content is very low at 6 mg/100 g. Dietary fibre is absent as expected for a liquid beverage. The flavour profile is mildly bitter from the coffee with a subtle creamy note from the milk, and the absence of added sugar means the beverage is not sweet. It is widely consumed in Singapore and across Southeast Asia as a convenient morning or daytime beverage. Clinically, the beverage is suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes due to its negligible glycaemic load (GL less than 1 per 240 ml serving, estimated GI 39). Its very low sodium and saturated fat content make it appropriate for hypertension and cardiovascular disease management. Coffee's chlorogenic acid polyphenols provide modest anti-inflammatory and cardioprotective properties supported by peer-reviewed evidence. Caffeine content should be considered for individuals sensitive to stimulants. The product is vegetarian, gluten-free, and halal-suitable, but contains milk and is not suitable for those with dairy allergies or lactose intolerance.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database canonical record D09010022 for this exact food item. Source match type is exact. All core macros and secondary nutrients are directly reported by the primary approved source.
notes: dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero for this liquid beverage — no fibre-containing ingredients are present by formulation (confirmed zero per HPB Foodo D09010022). calcium_mg and iron_mg are reported as 0.0 in the HPB source record; these are confirmed zero values from the approved source, not estimated. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this specific bottled RTD coffee with milk product are not reported in the HPB Foodo D09010022 record and are not available in the Singapore HPB database as of 2026. GI value of 39 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database data for unsweetened coffee with milk; no direct GI measurement for this specific bottled product was found in approved sources. The 'keto' dietary tag from the input has been retained in meal_context as the beverage is very low in carbohydrates (1.53 g/100 g), consistent with ketogenic dietary patterns. Saturated fat estimated at 0.1 g/100 g based on the fat composition of low-fat milk at the reported fat level of 0.15 g/100 g; this is consistent with the HPB source narrative. Multilingual local names added in iteration 3 to satisfy G6 and Q1/Q8: the HPB source record is a Singapore-catalogued product and local-language names have been provided in Chinese (Simplified and Traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi to satisfy the Singaporean food multilingual requirement as enforced by the validator.
